title: MiniMax-M1: Scaling Test-Time Compute Efficiently with Lightning Attention (CISPO)

summary.problem: Existing RL algorithms (PPO, GRPO, DAPO) clip token-level updates, which inadvertently eliminates gradient contributions from low-probability but critical reasoning tokens (e.g., 'However', 'Recheck'). After the first on-policy update, these tokens get clipped out entirely in subsequent off-policy rounds.
summary.method: CISPO clips importance sampling weights directly rather than token-level policy updates. The stop-gradient on the clipped IS weight ensures all tokens contribute gradients while IS weight only serves as a multiplicative scaling factor. Combined with Lightning Attention MoE architecture (456B params, 45.9B active).
summary.innovation: By moving clipping from token updates to IS weights with stop-gradient, CISPO preserves gradient signal from all tokens including rare reflective ones. Also: FP32 for LM output head to fix training-inference probability divergence; early truncation for pathological repetition.
summary.results: On Qwen2.5-32B, CISPO matches DAPO with only 50% training steps. MiniMax-M1: AIME 2024 86.0%, MATH-500 96.8%, LiveCodeBench 65.0%. Supports 1M token native context. Training: 3 weeks on 512 H800 GPUs.
summary.one_liner: CISPO 通过裁剪重要性采样权重（而非 token 级更新）解决了低概率推理 token 被错误裁掉的问题，训练效率比 DAPO 高一倍。
